title: Monkeypox Virus Multiplexed PCR Amplicon Sequencing (PrimalSeq)
author: Nicholas F.G. Chen*
materials: Reagents: Primers should be ordered lyophilized or resuspended (100 uM; recommended). Lyophilized primers should be resuspended to 100 uM in nuclease-free water.
*Safe Stopping Point: Amplified DNA may remain in the thermocycler at 4°C or stored at -20°C until ready to use*
Once the thermocycler reaches 10 °C , remove tubes/plates and spin down
Immediately add 10 µL of TSB to each tube/well to stop reaction. Pipette to mix. Cap tube (or seal plate) and place back in thermocycler

steps.title: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes
steps.instruction: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes: Master mix volumes are for one reaction and do not account for lost volume due to pipetting. Multiply volumes by reaction number accordingly.

steps.title: Label two sets of PCR tubes/plates...
steps.instruction: Label two sets of PCR tubes/plates for Pool 1 and Pool 2

steps.title: Add the following
steps.instruction: Add the following: 20 µL Pool 1 master mix to each Pool 1 tube/well 20 µL Pool 2 master mix to each Pool 2 tube/well

steps.title: Add 5 µL DNA to each...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL DNA to each tube in both sets Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge tubes/plates Be sure to include a negative PCR control (NTC; nuclease-free water) for each pool

steps.title: Place on thermocycler and run the...
steps.instruction: Place on thermocycler and run the following program (choose preheat lid option):

steps.title: Spin down PCR tubes/plates
steps.instruction: Spin down PCR tubes/plates

steps.title: Prepare the following master mix:
steps.instruction: Prepare the following master mix:

steps.title: In each PCR tube add
steps.instruction: In each PCR tube add: 30 µL Master mix 10 µL Pool 1 amplicons 10 µL Pool 2 amplicons For a total reaction volume of 50 µL per PCR tube/well Pooling of amplicons should be conducted on a dedicated post-PCR bench to prevent contamination

steps.title: Mix by pipetting up and down and briefly centrifuge
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting up and down and briefly centrifuge

steps.title: Place on thermocycler and run the...
steps.instruction: Place on thermocycler and run the following program (choose preheat lid option):

steps.title: Add 10 µL ST2 to each...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL ST2 to each tube/well, mix by pipetting up and down, and briefly centrifuge

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place on magnetic stand and wait...
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic stand and wait until liquid is clear (a few minutes)

steps.title: Remove and discard all supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all supernatant

steps.title: Remove tubes/plates from magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Remove tubes/plates from magnetic stand and add 100 µL TWB to each tube/well Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times and spin down. Be careful to not introduce bubbles .

steps.title: Repeat steps 4
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4.9 - 4.11. Leave the supernatant after the second wash so that the beads don’t dry out

steps.title: Prepare the following master mix:
steps.instruction: Prepare the following master mix:

steps.title: Place the tubes/plates with tagmented amplicons...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes/plates with tagmented amplicons on the magnetic stand and remove the supernatant once the liquid is clear

steps.title: Use a 20µL pipette to remove...
steps.instruction: Use a 20µL pipette to remove any residual TWB from tubes/wells

steps.title: Remove the tubes/plates from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the tubes/plates from the magnetic stand

steps.title: Add 40 µL master mix to each tube/well
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL master mix to each tube/well

steps.title: Add 10 µL dual-barcoded index adapters...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL dual-barcoded index adapters to each tube/well Note which set of indexes are used for each tube for bioinformatic processing

steps.title: Pipette up and down to mix and spin down
steps.instruction: Pipette up and down to mix and spin down

steps.title: Place on thermocycler and run the...
steps.instruction: Place on thermocycler and run the following program (choose preheat lid option):

steps.title: Place on magnetic stand and wait...
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic stand and wait until liquid is clear (a few minutes)

steps.title: Pipette up and down to mix...
steps.instruction: Pipette up and down to mix pooled libraries and briefly spin down

steps.title: Calculate the volume of ITB to reach a 0
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of ITB to reach a 0.6X beads:total pool volume ratio For example: Total pooled volume is 400 µL (10 samples x 40 µL/sample) then add 0.6 x 400 µL = 240 µL ITB

steps.title: Mix by pipetting up and down,...
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting up and down, briefly centrifuge, and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place on magnetic stand and wait...
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic stand and wait until liquid is clear (a few minutes)

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to a new tube...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to a new tube ( do not discard, this is your final library ) . This volume will be slightly lower than the total combined volume For example: 400µL+ 240µL = total 640µL > transfer 630µL

steps.title: Calculate the volume of beads to...
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of beads to add to the supernatant to attain a second clean-up beads:sample ratio of 0.9X: Vo = total volume of sample + beads from step 5.5 Vt = transferred volume of supernatant For example : 630µL of supernatant is transferred to a new tube Add 118µL of ITB to 630µL of transferred supernatant Calculation source : https://support.illumina.com/bulletins/2020/07/library-size-selection-using-sample-purification-beads.html

steps.title: Add beads to supernatant, mix by...
steps.instruction: Add beads to supernatant, mix by pipetting up and down, briefly centrifuge, and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place on magnetic stand and wait...
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic stand and wait until liquid is clear (a few minutes)

steps.title: Add 1000 µL 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL 80% EtOH

steps.title: Repeat steps 6
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 6.12 - 6.14

steps.title: Remove supernatant using a 20µL pipette...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant using a 20µL pipette to remove all residual EtOH

steps.title: Add 55 µL of RSB to...
steps.instruction: Add 55 µL of RSB to the tube, mix by pipetting up and down, and briefly centrifuge

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00

steps.title: Place on magnetic stand and wait...
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic stand and wait until liquid is clear (a few minutes)

steps.title: Transfer 50 µL of final pooled...
steps.instruction: Transfer 50 µL of final pooled library to a new 1.5mL tube

steps.title: Quantify library on a Qubit and...
steps.instruction: Quantify library on a Qubit and obtain fragment distribution using a Bioanalyzer/Tape Station Qubit SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf Bioanalyzer SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf

steps.title: Protocol validated on the MiSeq (v2...
steps.instruction: Protocol validated on the MiSeq (v2 kit running 2x150 nt reads) Note: For sequencing we recommend generating at least 1 million reads per sample for optimal sequencing coverage Sequencing may be performed on Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technologies sequencing platforms following standard protocols

steps.title: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing...
steps.instruction: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing a standard amplicon sequencing bioinformatics pipeline, including those employed for SARS-CoV-2 sequencing. If utilizing a SARS-CoV-2 based bioinformatics pipeline, adjustments may be required to account for the consensus genome length Optional bioinformatics pipeline: Terra.bio_TheiaCov_Illumina_PE_workflow V.1 files: Reference Sequence (MT903345): MPXV.reference.fasta Bed file: MPXV.primer.bed New V1.1 files: Reference Sequence (NC_003310): NC_003310.fasta Bed file: MPXV_primers_v1.1.bed

steps.title: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes
steps.instruction: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes: Master mix volumes are for one reaction and do not account for lost volume due to pipetting. Multiply volumes by reaction number accordingly.

steps.title: Label two sets of PCR tubes/plates...
steps.instruction: Label two sets of PCR tubes/plates for Pool 1 and Pool 2 For running multiple samples, a 96-well PCR plate may be used to prepare the master mixes

steps.title: Add the following
steps.instruction: Add the following: 20 µL Pool 1 master mix to each Pool 1 tube/well 20 µL Pool 2 master mix to each Pool 2 tube/well

steps.title: Add 5 µL DNA to each...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL DNA to each tube/well in both sets Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge Be sure to include a negative PCR control (NTC; nuclease-free water) for each pool

steps.title: Place on thermocycler and run the...
steps.instruction: Place on thermocycler and run the MPx Tiling PCR program :

steps.title: Prepare 400 µL of 80% EtOH per sample
steps.instruction: Prepare 400 µL of 80% EtOH per sample

steps.title: Allow AMPure XP beads 00:30:00 to...
steps.instruction: Allow AMPure XP beads 00:30:00 to reach room temperature and vortex to resuspend

steps.title: Add 50 µL of AMPure XP...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of AMPure XP beads to new PCR tubes/designated wells of a new plate and mix by pipetting 10 times

steps.title: Spin down PCR tubes/plates
steps.instruction: Spin down PCR tubes/plates

steps.title: In each designated PCR tube/well containing...
steps.instruction: In each designated PCR tube/well containing the AMPure XP beads add: 25 µL Pool 1 PCR reaction amplicons 25 µL Pool 2 PCR reaction amplicons Mix by pipetting 10 times Pooling of amplicons should be conducted on a dedicated post-PCR bench to prevent contamination Example of Post-PCR Tiling Pool Combination Plate Set-up For Multiple Samples:

steps.title: Ensure tube is capped (or plate...
steps.instruction: Ensure tube is capped (or plate is sealed with foil cover) and place on shaker at 1800 rpm for 00:10:00 at RT

steps.title: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand and wait until supernatant is clear (a few minutes)

steps.title: Carefully discard supernatant, being sure not...
steps.instruction: Carefully discard supernatant, being sure not to disturb the bead pellet

steps.title: Wash beads by covering pellet with...
steps.instruction: Wash beads by covering pellet with 200 µL of 80% EtOH

steps.title: Incubate for 00:00:30
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:00:30

steps.title: Remove EtOH with pipette and discard
steps.instruction: Remove EtOH with pipette and discard

steps.title: Repeat steps 4
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4.9-4.11 for a total of 2 washes

steps.title: Remove supernatant using clean pipette tips...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant using clean pipette tips to remove all residual 80% EtOH

steps.title: Keep tube/plate on the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Keep tube/plate on the magnetic stand and allow the bead to dry for 1-5 minutes Monitor pellet while drying. The pellet will have a matte appearance when it is dried. Over-drying will result in cracks forming in the pellet. Avoid over-drying, as it will make it difficult to resuspend the pellet as well as reduce yield. Immediately proceed to next

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from the magnetic stand,...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from the magnetic stand, add 32 µL of nuclease-free water to resuspend beads, and mix by pipetting

steps.title: Ensure tube is capped (or plate...
steps.instruction: Ensure tube is capped (or plate is sealed with foil cover) and place on orbital shaker at 1800 rpm for 00:05:00 at RT

steps.title: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand until...
steps.instruction: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand until supernatant is clear and colorless (~2 min)

steps.title: Remove 32 µL of supernatant and...
steps.instruction: Remove 32 µL of supernatant and transfer into a new tube/well (this constitutes the purified sample)

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: Purified DNA may be stored at -20°C*

steps.title: Quantify sample using the Qubit 1x...
steps.instruction: Quantify sample using the Qubit 1x dsDNA HS kit on the Qubit Flex Fluorometer Qubit SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf

steps.title: Record stock concentration (ng/µL) The ideal...
steps.instruction: Record stock concentration (ng/µL) The ideal level of DNA input is between 100-500 ng If sample concentration is between 20 to 100 ng/µl, add 5µl of sample to 25µl of dH 2 O. Make dilutions in a new 96 well plate If the sample concentration is lower than 20 ng/µl or higher than 100 ng/µl, calculate the volume necessary to input at least 100ng, and add the difference in volume of dH 2 O to make up 30µl total (e.g. if sample concentration is 10ng/µl, add 10µl of sample and 20µl of dH 2 O) If there is not enough DNA to achieve even 100ng of input, sequencing may not be feasible If Negative Extraction Controls are to be sequenced, the post-PCR concentration should be no higher than 5ng/µl. Negative extraction controls are added at 5µl of sample plus 25µl dH 2 O regardless of concentration No Template Controls (NTCs) may be added at this point by adding 30µl of dH 2 O to the appropriate well/tube

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: DNA may be stored at -20°C*

steps.title: Samples must be quantified, calculated, and diluted before proceeding
steps.instruction: Samples must be quantified, calculated, and diluted before proceeding

steps.title: In preparation for the Amplify Tag...
steps.instruction: In preparation for the Amplify Tag DNA step, thaw Enhance PCR Mix (EPM) and IDT for Illumina Nextera DNA UD Indexes plate on cold block. Gently vortex and quick spin before using Any index set (A-D) an be used; using a different set for each run is recommended to avoid read carryover.

steps.title: Thaw samples, Bead-Linked Transposomes (BLT), &...
steps.instruction: Thaw samples, Bead-Linked Transposomes (BLT), & Tagmentation Buffer (TB1) on a cold block. Vortex and quick spin before using

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5ml tube prepare the following tag master mix. Vortex master mix gently, spin down, and place in a cold block:

steps.title: In a new tube/designated wells of a new plate, add
steps.instruction: In a new tube/designated wells of a new plate, add: 20 µL Tag master mix 30 µL sample (purified DNA input with concentration adjusted to 100-500ng) Pipette 10 times to mix The calculated volumes of sample and dH2O may be added straight to the wells here, rather than diluting in a separate plate and transferring over.

steps.title: Ensure tube is capped (or plate...
steps.instruction: Ensure tube is capped (or plate is sealed with foil cover), gently vortex, and spin down

steps.title: Incubate at 55 °C for 00:15:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 55 °C for 00:15:00 and hold at 10 °C using the Flex Tagment Program:

steps.title: Vortex and quick spin Tagment Stop Buffer (TSB) before using
steps.instruction: Vortex and quick spin Tagment Stop Buffer (TSB) before using

steps.title: Remove tube/plate containing Tag master mix...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate containing Tag master mix and DNA input from thermocycler. Quick spin, and place on a cold block

steps.title: Incubate the reaction at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction at 37 °C for 00:15:00 and hold at 10 °C using the Flex Post-Tag program:

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from thermocycler and place...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from thermocycler and place on the magnetic stand and wait until supernatant is clear (~2 minutes)

steps.title: Remove and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from the magnetic stand and, using a deliberately slow pipetting technique, add 100 µL Tagment Wash Buffer (TWB) directly onto the beads and slowly pipette to mix 10 times Do not mix or vortex as this could denature the enzymes (proteins) as denoted by foaming

steps.title: Place the tube/plate back on the...
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate back on the magnetic stand and wait until the supernatant is clear (~2 minutes). Remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and add another 100 µL of TWB. Slowly pipette to mix

steps.title: Cap tube/seal plate and leave on magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Cap tube/seal plate and leave on magnetic stand. DO NOT remove TWB until step 9.1

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5 mL lo-bind tube, combine the following volumes to prepare the Flex Amplify master mix in the table below. Vortex and quick spin and place on cold block

steps.title: Remove and discard supernatant from the...
steps.instruction: Remove and discard supernatant from the tube/plate containing tagmented DNA and TWB

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and place on cold block
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and place on cold block

steps.title: Immediately add 40 µL of Flex...
steps.instruction: Immediately add 40 µL of Flex Amplify master mix to tube containing DNA

steps.title: Add 10 µL of assigned Illumina...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of assigned Illumina Nextera DNA UD Indexes to designated tube/well Note which set of indexes are used for each tube for bioinformatic processing IDT for Illumina DNA/RNA UD Index may also be used

steps.title: Shake tubes/plate at 1600 rpm for 00:01:00 and quick spin
steps.instruction: Shake tubes/plate at 1600 rpm for 00:01:00 and quick spin

steps.title: Incubate tubes/plate following the Flex Amplify program
steps.instruction: Incubate tubes/plate following the Flex Amplify program: The number of cycles (X) will be determined by the Total DNA Input for the samples being run, as listed in the chart below. As such, the samples on any given run will need to have a similar level of input in order to keep the cycle number applicable to all samples. If a sample’s input is different from the others being run, the Flex Amplify PCR step will have to be run on a separate thermocycler

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: Capped Tube/ Sealed Plate may be stored at 2-8°C for up to 30 days*

steps.title: Allow Sample Purification beads (SPB) to...
steps.instruction: Allow Sample Purification beads (SPB) to adjust to room temperature for least 00:30:00 . Vortex and invert to mix *IPB recently (2022) replaced Sample Purification Beads (SPB) in the Illumina DNA Prep kit

steps.title: Thaw Resuspension Buffer (RSB) to RT
steps.instruction: Thaw Resuspension Buffer (RSB) to RT. Vortex and quick spin before using

steps.title: Add 81 µL of undiluted IPB...
steps.instruction: Add 81 µL of undiluted IPB to new tubes/designated wells of a new plate

steps.title: Remove tubes/plate from thermocycler and quick spin
steps.instruction: Remove tubes/plate from thermocycler and quick spin. Place tubes/plate on magnetic stand for 2-5 mins or until clear

steps.title: Transfer 45 µL of sample supernatant,...
steps.instruction: Transfer 45 µL of sample supernatant, without disrupting the beads, from each tube/well into the corresponding tubes/wells containing IPB. Discard the used tubes/plate

steps.title: Cap tubes/seal plate with foil and...
steps.instruction: Cap tubes/seal plate with foil and shake at 1600 rpm for 00:01:00 on an orbital shaker

steps.title: Incubate on benchtop at RT for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate on benchtop at RT for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place tubes/plate on a magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place tubes/plate on a magnetic stand for ~2 minutes or until supernatant is clear

steps.title: Prepare fresh 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh 80% EtOH

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Wash beads by covering pellet with...
steps.instruction: Wash beads by covering pellet with 200 µL of 80% EtOH and incubate for 00:00:30 . Remove ethanol with pipette and discard

steps.title: Repeat step 10
steps.instruction: Repeat step 10.10 for a total of two washes

steps.title: Remove residual 80% EtOH with a clean pipette tip
steps.instruction: Remove residual 80% EtOH with a clean pipette tip

steps.title: Keep tubes/plate on magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Keep tubes/plate on magnetic stand and let bead dry for 1-5 minutes Monitor pellet while drying. The pellet will have a matte appearance when they are dried. Over-drying will result in cracks forming in the pellet. Avoid over-drying, as it will make it difficult to resuspend the pellet as well as reduce yield. Immediately proceed to next

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and resuspend beads with 32 µL of RSB. Cap tube/seal plate and shake at 1600 rpm for 00:01:00 on orbital shaker

steps.title: Let plate incubate on benchtop at RT for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Let plate incubate on benchtop at RT for 00:02:00

steps.title: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand for...
steps.instruction: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand for ~2 min until supernatant is clear. Remove 32 µL of supernatant and add to new tube/wells of a new plate The purified libraries are now ready for pooling and sequencing

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: Capped Tube/ Sealed Plate may be stored at -20°C*

steps.title: Libraries may be quantified via a...
steps.instruction: Libraries may be quantified via a Qubit prior to pooling or, alternatively, pooling may be done via equal volumes under the assumption of approximately equal library concentrations

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5mL tube, add 5 µL of each designated sample to create a library pool for sequencing

steps.title: Quantify library on a Qubit and...
steps.instruction: Quantify library on a Qubit and obtain fragment distribution using a Bioanalyzer/Tape Station Qubit SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf Bioanalyzer SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf

steps.title: Protocol validated on the MiSeq (v2...
steps.instruction: Protocol validated on the MiSeq (v2 kit running 2x150 nt reads) Note: For sequencing we recommend generating at least 1 million reads per sample for optimal sequencing coverage Sequencing may be performed on Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technologies sequencing platforms following standard protocols

steps.title: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing...
steps.instruction: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing a standard amplicon sequencing bioinformatics pipeline, including those employed for SARS-CoV-2 sequencing. If utilizing a SARS-CoV-2 based bioinformatics pipeline, adjustments may be required to account for the consensus genome length Optional bioinformatics pipeline: Terra.bio_TheiaCov_Illumina_PE_workflow Reference Sequence: MPXV.reference.fasta Bed file: MPXV.primer.bed

steps.title: If not already done, separate odd...
steps.instruction: If not already done, separate odd and even numbered primer pairs into two separate boxes. These will constitute the two pools For example: Primer Pool 1: 1 left, 1 right, 3 left, 3 right, etc. Primer Pool 2: 2 left, 2 right, 4 left, 4 right, etc.

steps.title: Label 164, 8-strip tubes with the...
steps.instruction: Label 164, 8-strip tubes with the corresponding odd-numbered primer name (e.g. 3 left)

steps.title: To each tube add 90 µL...
steps.instruction: To each tube add 90 µL of nuclease-free water This will comprise the 10X dilution to arrive at a final primer concentration of 10uM

steps.title: For each odd-numbered primer tube
steps.instruction: For each odd-numbered primer tube: Spin down Pipette 10 times to mix Add 10 µL to the corresponding labeled tube Pipette 10 times to mix

steps.title: After all 164 primers have been...
steps.instruction: After all 164 primers have been aliquoted, combine 10uL from each tube into a 2mL tube. This will be the odd-numbered primer pool To most efficiently pool all 164 primers, use a multi-channel pipette to remove 10uL from each 8-strip and pool into a new 8-strip. Then combine each of the pooled 8 strip tubes into a single 2mL tube

steps.title: Repeat steps 1.3-1.6 with the even -numbered primers ( NOTE
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 1.3-1.6 with the even -numbered primers ( NOTE: There will be two fewer primers in the even set than the odd)

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: Pooled Primers Can Be Stored at -20°C*

steps.title: Dilute primers to a working concentration...
steps.instruction: Dilute primers to a working concentration of 10uM with nuclease-free water (i.e. 1:10 dilution)

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: Pooled Primers Can Be Stored at -20°C*

steps.title: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes
steps.instruction: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes: Master mix volumes are for one reaction and do not account for lost volume due to pipetting. Multiply volumes by reaction number accordingly.

steps.title: Label two sets of PCR tubes/plates...
steps.instruction: Label two sets of PCR tubes/plates for Pool 1 and Pool 2

steps.title: Add the following
steps.instruction: Add the following: 20 µL Pool 1 master mix to each Pool 1 tube/well 20 µL Pool 2 master mix to each Pool 2 tube/well

steps.title: Add 5 µL DNA to each...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL DNA to each tube in both sets Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge tubes/plates Be sure to include a negative PCR control (NTC; nuclease-free water) for each pool

steps.title: Pipette up and down to mix...

steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL 80% EtOH

steps.title: Repeat steps 6
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 6.12 - 6.14

steps.title: Remove supernatant using a 20µL pipette...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant using a 20µL pipette to remove all residual EtOH

steps.title: Add 55 µL of RSB to...
steps.instruction: Add 55 µL of RSB to the tube, mix by pipetting up and down, and briefly centrifuge

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00

steps.title: Place on magnetic stand and wait...
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic stand and wait until liquid is clear (a few minutes)

steps.title: Transfer 50 µL of final pooled...
steps.instruction: Transfer 50 µL of final pooled library to a new 1.5mL tube

steps.title: Quantify library on a Qubit and...
steps.instruction: Quantify library on a Qubit and obtain fragment distribution using a Bioanalyzer/Tape Station Qubit SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf Bioanalyzer SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf

steps.title: Protocol validated on the MiSeq (v2...
steps.instruction: Protocol validated on the MiSeq (v2 kit running 2x150 nt reads) Note: For sequencing we recommend generating at least 1 million reads per sample for optimal sequencing coverage Sequencing may be performed on Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technologies sequencing platforms following standard protocols

steps.title: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing...
steps.instruction: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing a standard amplicon sequencing bioinformatics pipeline, including those employed for SARS-CoV-2 sequencing. If utilizing a SARS-CoV-2 based bioinformatics pipeline, adjustments may be required to account for the consensus genome length Optional bioinformatics pipeline: Terra.bio_TheiaCov_Illumina_PE_workflow V.1 files: Reference Sequence: MPXV.reference.fasta Bed file: MPXV.primer.bed New V1.1 files: Reference Sequence (NC_003310): NC_003310.fasta Bed file: MPXV_primers_v1.1.bed

steps.title: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes
steps.instruction: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes: Master mix volumes are for one reaction and do not account for lost volume due to pipetting. Multiply volumes by reaction number accordingly.

steps.title: Label two sets of PCR tubes/plates...
steps.instruction: Label two sets of PCR tubes/plates for Pool 1 and Pool 2 For running multiple samples, a 96-well PCR plate may be used to prepare the master mixes

steps.title: Add the following
steps.instruction: Add the following: 20 µL Pool 1 master mix to each Pool 1 tube/well 20 µL Pool 2 master mix to each Pool 2 tube/well

steps.title: Add 5 µL DNA to each...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL DNA to each tube/well in both sets Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge Be sure to include a negative PCR control (NTC; nuclease-free water) for each pool

steps.title: Place on thermocycler and run the...
steps.instruction: Place on thermocycler and run the MPx Tiling PCR program :

steps.title: Prepare 400 µL of 80% EtOH per sample
steps.instruction: Prepare 400 µL of 80% EtOH per sample

steps.title: Allow AMPure XP beads 00:30:00 to...
steps.instruction: Allow AMPure XP beads 00:30:00 to reach room temperature and vortex to resuspend

steps.title: Add 50 µL of AMPure XP...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of AMPure XP beads to new PCR tubes/designated wells of a new plate and mix by pipetting 10 times

steps.title: Spin down PCR tubes/plates
steps.instruction: Spin down PCR tubes/plates

steps.title: In each designated PCR tube/well containing...
steps.instruction: In each designated PCR tube/well containing the AMPure XP beads add: 25 µL Pool 1 PCR reaction amplicons 25 µL Pool 2 PCR reaction amplicons Mix by pipetting 10 times Pooling of amplicons should be conducted on a dedicated post-PCR bench to prevent contamination Example of Post-PCR Tiling Pool Combination Plate Set-up For Multiple Samples:

steps.title: Ensure tube is capped (or plate...
steps.instruction: Ensure tube is capped (or plate is sealed with foil cover) and place on shaker at 1800 rpm for 00:10:00 at RT

steps.title: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand and wait until supernatant is clear (a few minutes)

steps.title: Carefully discard supernatant, being sure not...
steps.instruction: Carefully discard supernatant, being sure not to disturb the bead pellet

steps.title: Wash beads by covering pellet with...
steps.instruction: Wash beads by covering pellet with 200 µL of 80% EtOH

steps.title: Incubate for 00:00:30
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:00:30

steps.title: Remove EtOH with pipette and discard
steps.instruction: Remove EtOH with pipette and discard

steps.title: Repeat steps 4
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4.9-4.11 for a total of 2 washes

steps.title: Remove supernatant using clean pipette tips...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant using clean pipette tips to remove all residual 80% EtOH

steps.title: Keep tube/plate on the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Keep tube/plate on the magnetic stand and allow the bead to dry for 1-5 minutes Monitor pellet while drying. The pellet will have a matte appearance when it is dried. Over-drying will result in cracks forming in the pellet. Avoid over-drying, as it will make it difficult to resuspend the pellet as well as reduce yield. Immediately proceed to next

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from the magnetic stand,...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from the magnetic stand, add 32 µL of nuclease-free water to resuspend beads, and mix by pipetting

steps.title: Ensure tube is capped (or plate...
steps.instruction: Ensure tube is capped (or plate is sealed with foil cover) and place on orbital shaker at 1800 rpm for 00:05:00 at RT

steps.title: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand until...
steps.instruction: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand until supernatant is clear and colorless (~2 min)

steps.title: Remove 32 µL of supernatant and...
steps.instruction: Remove 32 µL of supernatant and transfer into a new tube/well (this constitutes the purified sample)

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: Purified DNA may be stored at -20°C*

steps.title: Quantify sample using the Qubit 1x...
steps.instruction: Quantify sample using the Qubit 1x dsDNA HS kit on the Qubit Flex Fluorometer Qubit SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf

steps.title: Record stock concentration (ng/µL) The ideal...
steps.instruction: Record stock concentration (ng/µL) The ideal level of DNA input is between 100-500 ng If sample concentration is between 20 to 100 ng/µl, add 5µl of sample to 25µl of dH 2 O. Make dilutions in a new 96 well plate If the sample concentration is lower than 20 ng/µl or higher than 100 ng/µl, calculate the volume necessary to input at least 100ng, and add the difference in volume of dH 2 O to make up 30µl total (e.g. if sample concentration is 10ng/µl, add 10µl of sample and 20µl of dH 2 O) If there is not enough DNA to achieve even 100ng of input, sequencing may not be feasible If Negative Extraction Controls are to be sequenced, the post-PCR concentration should be no higher than 5ng/µl. Negative extraction controls are added at 5µl of sample plus 25µl dH 2 O regardless of concentration No Template Controls (NTCs) may be added at this point by adding 30µl of dH 2 O to the appropriate well/tube

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: DNA may be stored at -20°C*

steps.title: Samples must be quantified, calculated, and diluted before proceeding
steps.instruction: Samples must be quantified, calculated, and diluted before proceeding

steps.title: In preparation for the Amplify Tag...
steps.instruction: In preparation for the Amplify Tag DNA step, thaw Enhance PCR Mix (EPM) and IDT for Illumina Nextera DNA UD Indexes plate on cold block. Gently vortex and quick spin before using Any index set (A-D) an be used; using a different set for each run is recommended to avoid read carryover.

steps.title: Thaw samples, Bead-Linked Transposomes (BLT), &...
steps.instruction: Thaw samples, Bead-Linked Transposomes (BLT), & Tagmentation Buffer (TB1) on a cold block. Vortex and quick spin before using

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5ml tube prepare the following tag master mix. Vortex master mix gently, spin down, and place in a cold block:

steps.title: In a new tube/designated wells of a new plate, add
steps.instruction: In a new tube/designated wells of a new plate, add: 20 µL Tag master mix 30 µL sample (purified DNA input with concentration adjusted to 100-500ng) Pipette 10 times to mix The calculated volumes of sample and dH2O may be added straight to the wells here, rather than diluting in a separate plate and transferring over.

steps.title: Ensure tube is capped (or plate...
steps.instruction: Ensure tube is capped (or plate is sealed with foil cover), gently vortex, and spin down

steps.title: Incubate at 55 °C for 00:15:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 55 °C for 00:15:00 and hold at 10 °C using the Flex Tagment Program:

steps.title: Vortex and quick spin Tagment Stop Buffer (TSB) before using
steps.instruction: Vortex and quick spin Tagment Stop Buffer (TSB) before using

steps.title: Remove tube/plate containing Tag master mix...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate containing Tag master mix and DNA input from thermocycler. Quick spin, and place on a cold block

steps.title: Incubate the reaction at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction at 37 °C for 00:15:00 and hold at 10 °C using the Flex Post-Tag program:

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from thermocycler and place...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from thermocycler and place on the magnetic stand and wait until supernatant is clear (~2 minutes)

steps.title: Remove and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from the magnetic stand and, using a deliberately slow pipetting technique, add 100 µL Tagment Wash Buffer (TWB) directly onto the beads and slowly pipette to mix 10 times Do not mix or vortex as this could denature the enzymes (proteins) as denoted by foaming

steps.title: Place the tube/plate back on the...
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate back on the magnetic stand and wait until the supernatant is clear (~2 minutes). Remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and add another 100 µL of TWB. Slowly pipette to mix

steps.title: Cap tube/seal plate and leave on magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Cap tube/seal plate and leave on magnetic stand. DO NOT remove TWB until step 9.1

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5 mL lo-bind tube, combine the following volumes to prepare the Flex Amplify master mix in the table below. Vortex and quick spin and place on cold block

steps.title: Remove and discard supernatant from the...
steps.instruction: Remove and discard supernatant from the tube/plate containing tagmented DNA and TWB

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and place on cold block
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and place on cold block

steps.title: Immediately add 40 µL of Flex...
steps.instruction: Immediately add 40 µL of Flex Amplify master mix to tube containing DNA

steps.title: Add 10 µL of assigned Illumina...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of assigned Illumina Nextera DNA UD Indexes to designated tube/well Note which set of indexes are used for each tube for bioinformatic processing IDT for Illumina DNA/RNA UD Index may also be used

steps.title: Shake tubes/plate at 1600 rpm for 00:01:00 and quick spin
steps.instruction: Shake tubes/plate at 1600 rpm for 00:01:00 and quick spin

steps.title: Incubate tubes/plate following the Flex Amplify program
steps.instruction: Incubate tubes/plate following the Flex Amplify program: The number of cycles (X) will be determined by the Total DNA Input for the samples being run, as listed in the chart below. As such, the samples on any given run will need to have a similar level of input in order to keep the cycle number applicable to all samples. If a sample’s input is different from the others being run, the Flex Amplify PCR step will have to be run on a separate thermocycler

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: Capped Tube/ Sealed Plate may be stored at 2-8°C for up to 30 days*

steps.title: Allow Sample Purification beads (SPB) to...
steps.instruction: Allow Sample Purification beads (SPB) to adjust to room temperature for least 00:30:00 . Vortex and invert to mix *IPB recently (2022) replaced Sample Purification Beads (SPB) in the Illumina DNA Prep kit

steps.title: Thaw Resuspension Buffer (RSB) to RT
steps.instruction: Thaw Resuspension Buffer (RSB) to RT. Vortex and quick spin before using

steps.title: Add 81 µL of undiluted IPB...
steps.instruction: Add 81 µL of undiluted IPB to new tubes/designated wells of a new plate

steps.title: Remove tubes/plate from thermocycler and quick spin
steps.instruction: Remove tubes/plate from thermocycler and quick spin. Place tubes/plate on magnetic stand for 2-5 mins or until clear

steps.title: Transfer 45 µL of sample supernatant,...
steps.instruction: Transfer 45 µL of sample supernatant, without disrupting the beads, from each tube/well into the corresponding tubes/wells containing IPB. Discard the used tubes/plate

steps.title: Cap tubes/seal plate with foil and...
steps.instruction: Cap tubes/seal plate with foil and shake at 1600 rpm for 00:01:00 on an orbital shaker

steps.title: Incubate on benchtop at RT for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate on benchtop at RT for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place tubes/plate on a magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place tubes/plate on a magnetic stand for ~2 minutes or until supernatant is clear

steps.title: Prepare fresh 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh 80% EtOH

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Wash beads by covering pellet with...
steps.instruction: Wash beads by covering pellet with 200 µL of 80% EtOH and incubate for 00:00:30 . Remove ethanol with pipette and discard

steps.title: Repeat step 10
steps.instruction: Repeat step 10.10 for a total of two washes

steps.title: Remove residual 80% EtOH with a clean pipette tip
steps.instruction: Remove residual 80% EtOH with a clean pipette tip

steps.title: Keep tubes/plate on magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Keep tubes/plate on magnetic stand and let bead dry for 1-5 minutes Monitor pellet while drying. The pellet will have a matte appearance when they are dried. Over-drying will result in cracks forming in the pellet. Avoid over-drying, as it will make it difficult to resuspend the pellet as well as reduce yield. Immediately proceed to next

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from magnetic stand and resuspend beads with 32 µL of RSB. Cap tube/seal plate and shake at 1600 rpm for 00:01:00 on orbital shaker

steps.title: Let plate incubate on benchtop at RT for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Let plate incubate on benchtop at RT for 00:02:00

steps.title: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand for...
steps.instruction: Place tube/plate on magnetic stand for ~2 min until supernatant is clear. Remove 32 µL of supernatant and add to new tube/wells of a new plate The purified libraries are now ready for pooling and sequencing

steps.title: *Safe Stopping Point
steps.instruction: *Safe Stopping Point: Capped Tube/ Sealed Plate may be stored at -20°C*

steps.title: Libraries may be quantified via a...
steps.instruction: Libraries may be quantified via a Qubit prior to pooling or, alternatively, pooling may be done via equal volumes under the assumption of approximately equal library concentrations

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5mL tube, add 5 µL of each designated sample to create a library pool for sequencing

steps.title: Quantify library on a Qubit and...
steps.instruction: Quantify library on a Qubit and obtain fragment distribution using a Bioanalyzer/Tape Station Qubit SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf Bioanalyzer SOP: Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit Guide.pdf

steps.title: Protocol validated on the MiSeq (v2...
steps.instruction: Protocol validated on the MiSeq (v2 kit running 2x150 nt reads) Note: For sequencing we recommend generating at least 1 million reads per sample for optimal sequencing coverage Sequencing may be performed on Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technologies sequencing platforms following standard protocols

steps.title: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing...
steps.instruction: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing a standard amplicon sequencing bioinformatics pipeline, including those employed for SARS-CoV-2 sequencing. If utilizing a SARS-CoV-2 based bioinformatics pipeline, adjustments may be required to account for the consensus genome length Optional bioinformatics pipeline: Terra.bio_TheiaCov_Illumina_PE_workflow Reference Sequence: MPXV.reference.fasta Bed file: MPXV.primer.bed
